merge.diff:
diff --git a/src/jinja2/filters.py b/src/jinja2/filters.py index 80ea6504..71c113cc 100644 --- a/src/jinja2/filters.py +++ b/src/jinja2/filters.py @@ -1163,6 +1163,8 @@ def sync_do_groupby( value: "t.Iterable[V]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + case_sensitive: bool = False, + with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": """Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's :func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for @@ -1203,6 +1205,23 @@ def sync_do_groupby(
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %} + By default, strings are compared case-insensitively. Set + ``case_sensitive`` to ``True`` to compare case-sensitively. + + Set ``with_counts`` to ``True`` to include the number of items in + each group. ``groupby`` will then yield ``(grouper, list, count)`` + tuples instead. + + .. sourcecode:: html+jinja + +
      {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %} +
    • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
      • + {% endfor %}
    + + .. versionchanged:: 3.1 + Added the ``case_sensitive`` parameter. Added the ``with_counts`` + parameter. + .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. @@ -1210,10 +1229,21 @@ def sync_do_groupby( The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) - return [ - _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) - ] + sort_expr = make_attrgetter( + environment, + attribute, + default=default, + postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, + ) + out = [] + for _, group in groupby(sorted(value, key=sort_expr), sort_expr): + values = list(group) + out.append(_GroupTuple(expr(values[0]), values)) + + if with_counts: + return [(grouper, items, len(items)) for grouper, items in out] + + return out @async_variant(sync_do_groupby) # type: ignore @@ -1222,12 +1252,26 @@ async def do_groupby( value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + case_sensitive: bool = False, + with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) - return [ - _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) - ] + sort_expr = make_attrgetter( + environment, + attribute, + default=default, + postprocess=ignore_case if not case_sensitive else None, + ) + values = await auto_to_list(value) + out = [] + for _, group in groupby(sorted(values, key=sort_expr), sort_expr): + group_values = list(group) + out.append(_GroupTuple(expr(group_values[0]), group_values)) + + if with_counts: + return [(grouper, items, len(items)) for grouper, items in out] + + return out @pass_environment
